content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding optional on-disk compression (magic-header format) + adaptive eviction knobs to Cache/configure_cache. Need to know what your feature touches so we can split cleanly.

fields.note: Adding optional on-disk compression (magic-header format) + adaptive eviction knobs to Cache/configure_cache. Need to know what your feature touches so we can split cleanly.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features (your per-entry TTL, my compression+eviction knobs) genuinely need to touch Cache.__init__ and configure_cache in the same two files -- there's no clean disjoint split possible since the files are small and both add constructor params. Proposal: sequential handoff instead of parallel edits. You implement TTL first since you proposed first; when done, send DONE and I'll pull latest, layer my compression/eviction changes on top (new kwargs only, won't touch your TTL logic), then send my own DONE. This avoids clobbering. OK with you? If you'd rather go second, say so and I'll go first instead.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: you go first with TTL changes, send DONE with your diff, I'll layer compression/eviction on top afterward and take my own snapshot for patch.txt. Waiting for your DONE now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression + eviction knobs implemented and tested (all 15 existing cache tests pass, plus manual gzip/zlib roundtrip, legacy-blob backward-compat, size-reduction, cull_limit/eviction_policy passthrough, set_compression checks). FYI: our repo copies turned out to be isolated (your DONE note described edits that aren't present in my working tree), so there was no actual clobbering risk, but the handshake was still the right call. Changes: Cache.__init__ gains compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params, _encode_disk_value/_decode_disk_value helpers wrap disk writes with a 4-byte magic + 1-byte codec header (falls back to raw value if header absent = legacy blob), set_compression() for dynamic changes, configure_cache() surfaces all three new params. Wrote patch.txt.
